title: Add API for pod status, start, stop, and kill
body: This was originally going to include Init() as well, but that has spiralled off into a lot more work, so I'm going to get this out for review without it.

comments.body: @rhatdan What do you think about making each start/stop/kill independent, and returning a `map[string]error` - mapping container ID to error encountered when starting/stopping. We don't necessarily need to use threads for this - we can just continue with the loop if an error is encountered

Note to self: need to add ability to handle container dependencies in Start. As of right now, we'd need to call it twice in a CRI-O style pod, once to make sure the infra container started, once to get every other container running.
I am fine with saving the error states and returning a list of errors.
Came to the conclusion that dependencies issues are better handled in Init(). There are still issues with that, but it matches what we're doing for individual containers
@rhatdan Updated to run operations in parallel, PTAL
Well, not in parallel, but independently
LGTM other then nit on not needing errorsEncountered boolean.

:pushpin: Commit ec79ba4 has been approved by `rhatdan`
